info.title: Workshop API
info.description:
HTTP API for integrations that work on behalf of bike workshops on Workshop — jobs, customers, services, chat, and webhooks. Authenticate with your API key (`Bearer bbk_live_...`). Writes require `Idempotency-Key`. JSON uses `snake_case` and ids such as `org_`, `job_`, `cus_`.

**Guides:** [Getting started](/guides/getting-started) · [Platform overview](/guides/overview) · [Authentication](/guides/authentication) · [Rate limiting](/guides/rate-limiting) · [Retries & idempotency](/guides/retries-and-idempotency) · [Errors & pagination](/guides/errors-and-pagination) · [Resource ids](/guides/public-ids) · [Conventions](/guides/conventions) · [Availability and dates](/guides/availability) · [Part authorisations](/guides/part-authorisations) · [Back orders](/guides/back-orders) · [Chat](/guides/chat) · [Webhooks](/guides/webhooks) · [Webhook payloads](/guides/webhook-payloads) · [Changelog](/guides/changelog)

get.description: Lists customer assets for workshops allowed by the API key. Optional `business_id` narrows results to one workshop. Use named filters to look up assets before booking jobs.

parameters.name: bike_type
parameters.description: Exact match on bike category (bikes only). Allowed values: bmx, e_cargo, e_gravel, e_road, electric_bike, generic_bike, gravel, hybrid, kids_bike, mountain_bike, other, road_bike, single_speed, static_bike, triathlon_bike.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort field. Allowed values: `name`, `created_at`. Default: `name`. Prefix with `-` for descending order (for example `-name`).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of resources to return (1–100, default 10).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque page cursor from a previous list response's `pagination.next_cursor`.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer Workshop API key. Example: Bearer bbk_live_...

parameters.name: X-Bikebook-Request-Id
parameters.description: Optional request id supplied by the client for support correlation.

get.description: Lists back orders for workshops allowed by the API key. Optional `business_id` narrows results to one workshop. Optional filters: `status` (`needs_ordering`, `waiting_on_delivery`, `received`), `stock_variation_id`, `job_id`, `invoice_id`, `customer_id`, `sku`, and `source` (`general`, `job`, `invoice`). Use comma-separated values for `status` to match any supplied value.

get.description: Returns enabled services in enabled categories with `external_and_internal` visibility only. Admin-locked or internal-only services are omitted. Supports cursor pagination via `limit` and `cursor` (max 100 per page). Optional `name` matches service name, description, or category (substring). Optional `sort` accepts `order`, `name`, `price`, or `category`. Prefix with `-` for descending order.

get.description: Returns bookable calendar dates as `YYYY-MM-DD` (no time or timezone). Optional `from` and `to` default to workshop-local today and 42 days ahead. Date arrays are empty when the related fulfilment mode is disabled on the business profile.

parameters.description: Inclusive start date (`YYYY-MM-DD`). Optional; defaults to workshop-local today on availability and next_available_slot.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Inclusive end date (`YYYY-MM-DD`). Optional; defaults to `from` + 42 days on availability, or `from` + 90 days on next_available_slot.

parameters.name: business_id

post.description: Requires `job.write` and a unique `Idempotency-Key` header. The workshop is determined by `customer.id`: the customer must already exist and belong to a business your API key can access. If your key spans multiple workshops, use a customer id scoped to the target workshop. Each asset entry must supply either an existing `assets[].id` or a request-local `client_reference` for inline asset creation. Each work line must supply either `asset_id` or `asset_client_reference` matching an asset on the request. The request body is workflow-shaped: customer, assets, work_lines, schedule, notifications, and integration_options. Send the body as `application/json` (not JSON Patch). See the development guide for minimal and full JSON examples.
